title: First Jedi Purge

}}
{{Quote|The Jedi Temple on Coruscant lies empty. The waters in the Room of a Thousand Fountains have fallen still, in reverence to the fallen Jedi… and those now lost.|[[Kreia]]|<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />}}
The '''First Jedi Purge''' was the genocide that saw the near—destruction of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] by the [[Sith Triumvirate]] from [[3954 BBY]] until [[3951 BBY]], which saw the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|disappear]] from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]— as the Order [[Lost Jedi|fought]] for its survival. 

During the [[Sith Civil War]], circa 3954 BBY, [[Kreia|Darth Traya]], [[Darth Nihilus]], and [[Darth Sion]] took command of the remnants of [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. After the exile of Traya, Sion utilized his [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassins]] to assassinate Jedi, while Nihilus would consume the life of entire planets in search of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Nihilus's final attack was on the [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] world of [[Katarr/Legends|Katarr]]; nearly all of the remaining hundred Jedi died at a [[Conclave at Katarr|meeting]] being held there.

Despite nearly eliminating the Jedi Order, the Sith ultimately failed, as the Order had survived in exile, where [[Meetra Surik]] had trained a new generation of Jedi, but also [[Battle of Telos IV#Destruction of the Ravager|personally]] [[Battle of Malachor#The Battle|killed]] all three leaders of the Sith Triumvirate in combat. After the conclusion of the purge, the Jedi who Surik trained proved essential to the future of the Jedi Order. 

==Origins==
{{Quote|The Jedi Civil War destroyed the Jedi. By the war's end, barely a hundred Jedi remained. Many fell in battle… and many more were seduced by Revan's teachings.|Kreia|<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />}}
By the year [[3956 BBY]], the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] had weathered one of the most turbulent periods in its long history—three galaxy-spanning [[Old Sith Wars|wars]] that occurred in the short span of fifty years, two of which had been orchestrated by fallen members of the Order itself. In the aftermath of those devastating conflicts, the Order had dwindled and weakened to the point that it had become a shadow of its former self. Thousands of Jedi were lost in the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], the [[Mandalorian Wars]], and [[Jedi Civil War]], and those conflicts erupted across the galaxy in such close succession, that the Jedi were not given enough time to rebuild their numbers; the Order simply could not sustain such heavy losses over such a short period of time.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

The Order's problems were compounded by the fact that the Jedi faced a dramatic loss of knowledge stemming from the destruction of critical centers of learning, including the [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Great Jedi Library]] on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] and the [[Jedi Enclave]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. Too many important [[Holocron/Legends|holocrons]], records, and histories had been scattered, lost, or looted during the wars. As a result, there were too few experienced Jedi to instruct aspiring students and an inadequate knowledge base with which to teach them.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

By the end of the Jedi Civil War, the already depleted Jedi Order had been all but wiped out. Less than one hundred individuals survived to rebuild. As the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] hovered on the brink of collapse, the Jedi Order slowly began to deteriorate. Also, a redeemed [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] left Republic space, denying both the Republic and Jedi someone to rally around to rebuild. An intense climate of disillusionment permeated the Jedi ranks, as the few surviving members faced an unprecedented level of [[Anti-Jedi sentiment/Legends|anger and resentment]] from the Republic citizenship who had begun to fear their former heroes. The Jedi themselves were losing faith in the Order, as they began to ask themselves how and why so many of their number had fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] over the years. Even members of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] began to question the Jedi teachings.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

===The Sith Lords===
{{Quote|Only a handful of us remained after the Jedi Civil War, barely a hundred in number. Then even that hundred began to disappear, in places where the Force seemed blind. The only pattern we determined [was] that when Jedi gathered, they were seen no more. At the last Jedi conclave on Katarr, the entire planet was wiped out. An entire race, destroyed… because the Jedi chose to gather there. It was only then that we realized that we were facing something far more powerful than we knew how to fight.|[[Zez-Kai Ell]]|<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />}}
[[File:Katarrdevastation.jpg|thumb|left|230px|The devastation of Katarr]]After the death of [[Darth Malak]] and the destruction of the [[Star Forge]]<ref name="KotOR game">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>, the infrastructure of the Sith Empire had disintegrated. Most of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] who had survived the war wiped themselves out in [[Sith Civil War|struggles]] for dominance, yet scattered remnants of Revan's Empire remained entrenched in secret places of power. With time, they regrouped, and new leaders emerged to guide them. On the ancient Sith world of [[Malachor V]], Revan's secret sect of [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassins]] still lingered. In the heart of the [[Trayus Academy]], the [[Trayus Core]], the Sith Lords [[Kreia|Darth Traya]], [[Darth Nihilus]], and [[Darth Sion]] developed a new strategy. Waging a shadow war, the new [[Sith Triumvirate]] battled with only one goal in mind: the complete and utter extermination of the Jedi. They manipulated events from the darkness, causing local conflicts on key Republic planets. These worlds, if left to fall, would mean a quick and decisive end to the Republic.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Reeling from crippling losses sustained during the Jedi Civil War, the Order hobbled on the brink of extinction. The Jedi Council's authority had completely broken down, and satellite [[Jedi academy|academies]] were near-empty. It was a dark and chaotic time for the Order. A steady stream of Jedi, including the [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Jedi Watchmen]] overseeing several worlds, stepped down, renounced the Order, and were never heard from again. Entire Jedi gatherings began to vanish, leaving behind a strange [[Disturbance in the Force/Legends|disturbance in the Force]] that only a few members of the Jedi Council could recognize. In time, the Jedi began to suspect that they were being hunted. At last, the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Atris]] summoned many of the most powerful and influential of the remaining Jedi Masters to a secret [[Conclave at Katarr|conclave]] on the [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] world of [[Katarr/Legends|Katarr]] under the pretext of discussing the emerging threat. But the conclave was not as secret as the Jedi thought.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" /><ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

Atris leaked information about the conclave to the greater galaxy in an attempt to draw whoever was attacking them out of the shadows. As a result, Darth Nihilus arrived as the Jedi assembled and annihilated every living thing upon the planet, both the Jedi and the [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] colonists. The sole survivor of the attack was the Miraluka [[Visas Marr]], whom Nihilus captured and trained as his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]]. After that shocking event, the remaining Jedi Council members, Masters [[Vrook Lamar]], [[Kavar]], [[Zez-Kai Ell]], and [[Lonna Vash]], decided to go into exile. In so doing, they hoped that the mysterious and secretive new Sith threat would be lulled into a false victory and reveal itself.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

==The Jedi Exile==
{{Quote|We are at war, but it is unlike any war we have fought before—we have yet to even meet our attackers in battle. Somehow our enemy is targeting us through the Force, striking, then retreating to the shadows. The gathering of any Jedi seems to attract them like a beacon.|Vrook Lamar|<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />}}
===Return to known space===
By the year [[3951 BBY]], almost all the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] had been eliminated. One of the few survivors was [[Meetra Surik]], also known as the Jedi Exile. Surik—who had spent time in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] and the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], having been banished from the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] for following [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] to fight in the [[Mandalorian Wars]]—returned to [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] space in 3951 BBY. The [[Harbinger (Hammerhead-class)|''Harbinger'']], a Republic cruiser bound for [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], had been ordered by the Republic admiral [[Carth Onasi]], a veteran of the [[Mandalorian Wars]] and the [[Jedi Civil War]], to abort the planned trip and head to the Outer Rim instead, in order to pick up Surik. Then bound for [[Telos IV]], the ''Harbinger'' suddenly received a distress call and, upon reporting it and the distressed vessel's ID, was ordered to assist the vessel.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Upon their arrival the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the ''Harbinger'' discovered both the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' and the Sith warship seemingly adrift. He ordered a boarding crew onto the Sith vessel and the ''Ebon Hawk'' to bring aboard any survivors. The Sith warship was found to be completely empty - a "ghost ship" as one unnerved Republic soldier remarked - save for the 'corpse' of the then unknown Lord Sion, which was brought aboard. Over the next few days, the crew reported feelings of "being watched" and unexplained mishaps occurring, such as systems suddenly dying on them, climaxing when Lord Sion awoke and Republic officers being killed by cloaked Sith assassins that had secretly boarded the ''Harbinger'' when the ship had attached an umbilical to the 'empty' Sith warship.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

While the Sith were taking control of the ''Harbinger'', the ''Ebon Hawk'' docked with the vessel, extracted Surik and attempted to depart. The ''Harbinger'' had been completely taken over by the Sith at this time and fired on the small freighter, heavily damaging it as it jumped to hyperspace, rendering everyone aboard unconscious as a result.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

===Peragus===
{{Main|Skirmish at Peragus II}}
Several days later, Surik woke up from a stupor in the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] facility and found an old woman who had been left for dead. This woman later revealed herself to be a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] named [[Kreia]]. She guided Surik through their escape from the mining facility. They found the facility to be completely abandoned, except for malfunctioning [[mining droid]]s, an [[HK-50 series assassin droid|HK-50]] [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], and an [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] named [[Atton Rand]].<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Before long, however, the Sith Lord who had attacked the ''Harbinger'' arrived. Kreia, the Exile, and Atton escaped with T3-M4 onboard the ''Ebon Hawk'', setting a course for Telos IV—the only available destination in the [[Peragus system/Legends|Peragus]] navigational charts. While being pursued by the Sith in the [[Peragus asteroid field|asteroid field]] around Peragus, laser fire from the ''Harbinger'' ignited the volatile fuel within the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]]. The resulting explosion destroyed both the facility and the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Peragus II]] itself, although the ''Ebon Hawk'' and the Sith on-board the ''Harbinger'' were able to escape.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

===Telos===
After arriving at the massive [[Citadel Station]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around Telos, the crew of the ''Ebon Hawk'' were detained for the destruction of the Peragus mining facility. They were soon cleared of any crime—but not before the ''Ebon Hawk'', with T3-M4 on-board, had been stolen and flown to the Telosian surface. After being refused a new vessel by the [[Telos Security Force]], the three utilized alternate transportation to get down to the surface, where they were shot down by [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] air-defense turrets. They were pulled out of the burning vessel by a [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] named [[Bao-Dur]], who was responsible for designing Telos' planetary shields. While on the surface, they learned that the ''Ebon Hawk'' had been taken to the old planetary aqueducts in the polar region of the planet. Stealing a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] from an abandoned military compound, they traveled to the aqueducts and were once again shot down, this time by three HK-50 [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droids]]. After defeating the droids, Atton, Kreia and Surik (Bao-Dur was knocked unconscious by the crash) entered the polar aqueduct system.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

There, they found themselves once again being detained by [[Brianna|the Last Handmaiden]] and her [[Handmaiden Sisters|sisters]]. They had entered the secret [[Telosian Jedi Academy]]. Surik was granted an audience with [[Atris]], the Jedi Master controlling the facility. After a heated debate over what Surik should have and did do during the Mandalorian Wars, she and her party were granted leave and left with the ''Ebon Hawk'' and T3-M4.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

After leaving the academy, T3 revealed that he had managed to download Atris's database and navigational charts while at the academy, including a recording of Surik's trial and records of where the last five remaining Jedi Masters were hiding: Atris on Telos, [[Kavar]] on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], [[Vrook Lamar]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], [[Zez-Kai Ell]] on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] and [[Lonna Vash]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Surik, wanting to know why she had been exiled, traveled to these worlds in search of the Jedi in an attempt to unite them against the Sith and to find the answers to her questions.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

==The Lost Jedi==
===Dantooine===
{{Main|Battle of Khoonda}}
During her search for the remaining masters, Surik came to Dantooine to search for Master Vrook. When she arrived, she found that much had changed since she left for the Mandalorian Wars: the [[Jedi Enclave]] had been destroyed, and Dantooine was barely surviving as a planetary government. The remaining citizens had pulled together and built a new government based at the old [[Matale estate]], that was known as [[Khoonda]]. After meeting with the administrator, she journeyed into the ruins of the Enclave. There, she found out that Vrook had been captured by the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] on the planet.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Surik found and freed Vrook, but Vrook said he had allowed himself to be captured to avoid a conflict with Khoonda and the mercenaries. Now that they had lost Vrook, the mercenaries would launch a full-scale assault on the weak government. Surik chose to help Khoonda, and under her military leadership the [[Khoonda Militia]] defeated the mercenaries.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

===Nar Shaadaa===
{{Main|Mission to Nar Shaddaa (First Jedi Purge)}}
[[File:Narshaddaa.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' on Nar Shaddaa.]]
The second stop on their search was the moon [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]. Nar Shaddaa was the ''"gaping maw of [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]]."'' The massive population and large cityscape of the moon made it easy for one to hide and lay low for a while. Unfortunately, Surik did just the opposite. In her attempt to find Jedi Master [[Zez-Kai Ell]], Surik made an effort to cause much anger and distress to the [[Exchange]], and get the bounty hunters off her back. When Meetra was captured, the famous bounty hunter [[Mira]] joined up with Meetra's party and they rescued her on G0-T0's yacht. During the rescue, the yacht's cloaking was sabotaged and one of the local Hutts [[Vogga]], whose freighters were being raided by G0-T0's yacht, quickly seized the opportunity and destroyed it, right as the Ebon Hawk escaped.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Finally, Surik came back to Nar Shaddaa and found the Jedi Master. The Master confessed that he felt that the Jedi Order had much to take responsibility for, and their inability to do so at Surik's trial caused him to leave. He answered her questions and taught her a lightsaber form. He then agreed to go to Dantooine and wait for her there.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

===Onderon===
{{Main|Onderon Civil War}}
Surik's journey also took her to Onderon where Master Kavar, who was also an old friend, was staying as an advisor to [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Talia (Onderon)|Talia]]. But she found that, like Dantooine, Onderon also had problems: Many people on Onderon blamed the Republic for all the suffering that had come during the recent wars. Taking advantage of this, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Vaklu]], Talia's cousin, began to round up followers by saying Onderon should break away from the Republic and become independent again. His plans were to ultimately kill Talia and make himself king.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

After nearly being shot down by Vaklu's forces, Surik landed on [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]], one of Onderon's [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. There, she [[Skirmish on Dxun|found]] the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]], under the command of [[Canderous Ordo|Mandalore the Preserver]], were rebuilding. She was able to ally with the Mandalorians and get safe passage to Onderon. But her meeting with Kavar was cut short by Vaklu's troops. She left the planet with Kavar's word that he would contact her again.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

By the time Kavar was able to contact Surik, Vaklu was ready to strike. He launched a civil war in the streets of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]]. When Surik heard of this, she headed to Dxun. Surik, along with the Mandalorians, launched a counter-attack from Dxun and succeeded in defeating Vaklu before Talia could be defeated.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

===Korriban===
{{Expandsect}}
Surik also traveled to the Sith world of Korriban to search for Master Lonna Vash. Shortly after the ''Ebon Hawk'' touched down on the planet, Darth Sion killed Vash in the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]]. Surik then encountered Sion while searching the academy for Vash. As they [[Duel in the Korriban Academy (First Jedi Purge)|dueled]], Surik gained the upper hand, striking down Sion several times. Unfortunately for Surik, the Lord of Pain used his Dark Side powers to heal himself each time. Finally Kreia contacted Surik telepathically urging her to flee, telling Surik that this was a battle she could not win.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

After escaping from Sion, Surik discovered the lost [[Tomb of Ludo Kressh]], where she confronted and defeated several visions from her past and present. These included Malak recruiting her to join the [[Revanchist]]s in the Mandalorian Wars, the [[Battle of Dxun]], a fight between Kreia and her other companions, and Revan. She emerged from the tomb stronger in the belief her decisions had made her the Jedi she was.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

===Meeting with the Council===
After locating and conversing with all of the remaining Jedi Masters, the Exile returned to Dantooine to find the rebuilt [[Jedi Enclave]]. There, Masters Vrook, Kavar and Zez-Kai Ell (Master Lonna Vash had already been killed by the Sith) had formed a council in which they would decide her fate. Right after they reestablished the Exile's banishment and just before they could strip her of the Force once again, Kreia stormed into the meeting. The Exile, held in stasis by the Masters, was left helpless to defend them as Kreia put the Jedi Masters through the [[Force drain/Legends|same suffering the Exile had felt]] on [[Malachor V]], leaving them not only dead, but void of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

After she left, Surik finally regained her strength and departed for Telos in search of Kreia, who had once again assumed the title of Darth Traya. There the Exile was again forced to confront Atris, who had been corrupted by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] by the [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocrons]] at the secret academy. After the Exile defeated her, she was able to persuade Atris to forgive her and helped herself see the folly of her beliefs. Atris then told the Exile that Traya had fled to Malachor V to wait for their final confrontation. Afterwards, Atris declared herself an ex-Jedi and went into exile to try to find understanding.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

===The Battle of Telos IV===
{{Main|Battle of Telos IV}}
Shortly thereafter, a Sith [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] headed by [[Darth Nihilus]] arrived at Telos, having been drawn to the academy in the polar region. With the assistance of the Mandalorians and [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] Canderous Ordo, the Exile boarded and engaged Nihilus aboard his ship, the [[Ravager (Centurion-class)|''Ravager'']], to keep him from destroying Telos in order to feed his hunger. With the assistance of [[Visas Marr]] and Canderous, the Exile defeated him and headed to Malachor V.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

===The end of the Purge===
{{Main|Battle of Malachor}}
[[File:MalachorVDestroyed.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Malachor V is destroyed.]]
Having succeeded in defending Telos and defeating Darth Nihilus, the Exile and her associates made their way to Malachor V. After crash-landing on the surface of the planet, the Exile proceeded to the [[Trayus Academy]], battling her way through [[storm beast]]s, Sith warriors, and assassins before confronting Darth Sion himself. After an intense and prolonged duel, the Exile finally brought Sion to the realization that Kreia was using him and didn't care what happened to him. Having been persuaded to let go of his hatred—the only thing that kept him alive—Sion finally succumbed to his numerous injuries. The Exile then made her way to the [[Trayus Core]], where she confronted Darth Traya.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

After a fierce battle, the Exile succeeded in defeating the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] but spared her life. Traya refused to give up however, and attacked the Exile again. But the Exile was able to defeat her again. Before she gave her life to the Force, Kreia gave the Exile information about Revan's location in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] and about the fates of the Exile's allies and the planets they had visited. Shortly after this exchange, [[Bao-Dur's remote]] reactivated the [[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]] in order to [[Battle of Malachor|completely destroy the planet]]. The crew of the badly damaged ''Ebon Hawk'' retrieved the Exile from the Core and escaped the planet.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

With the defeat of the [[Sith Triumvirate]] and the "restoration" of the Jedi Order, the Exile left the Republic in the hands of her [[Lost Jedi]] and began her journey to find Revan in the Unknown Regions.<ref name="Revan">''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]''</ref>

==Casualties==
*[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Dorak]]: Killed by [[Darth Nihilus]] on [[Katarr/Legends|Katarr]].<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*Master [[Zez-Kai Ell]]: Killed by [[Kreia]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]].<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*Master [[Kavar]]: Killed by Kreia on Dantooine.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*Master [[Vrook Lamar]]: Killed by Kreia on Dantooine.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*Master [[Zhar Lestin]]: Killed on Katarr.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*Master [[Vandar Tokare]]: Killed on Katarr.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*Master [[Lonna Vash]]: Killed by [[Darth Sion]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]].<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*Jedi Knight [[Nebelish]]: Killed in the [[Tomb of Ludo Kressh|Tomb]] of [[Ludo Kressh]] on Korriban.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*[[Unidentified Jedi (Tomb of Ludo Kressh)]]: Killed in the Tomb of Ludo Kressh on Korriban.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

==Known survivors==
{{ScrollBox|
*Master [[Atris]]<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*[[Bao-Dur]]<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*[[Ood Bnar]]: Survived in exile on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] until his death in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].<ref name="Dark Empire II 3">[[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]]</ref>
*[[Brianna]]<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" /><ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords">Although canonically Brianna cannot join the player's party in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', ''[http://www.wizards.com/default.asp?x=starwars/article/KOTORminispreview5 Star Wars Miniatures: Knights of the Old Republic Preview 5]'' confirmed that she did travel with the [[Meetra Surik|Jedi Exile]]. It is therefore assumed that she would have been trained in the use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the Exile—as occurs when the player character is male—and, according to [[Kreia]]'s prophecy, taken [[Atris]]' role as [[Jedi historian]].</ref>
*[[Meetra Surik]]<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />: Survived until her death on [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]] in [[3950 BBY]].<ref name="Revan">''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]''</ref>
*[[Halbret]]: Survived until her death in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]].<ref name="Endgame">''[[Endgame]]''</ref>
*[[Deesra Luur Jada]]<ref name="Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>
*[[Visas Marr]]<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*[[Mical]]<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*[[Mira]]<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*[[Celeste Morne]]<ref name="DT11">[[Dark Times 11|''Dark Times'' 11]]</ref> Survived until her death in [[137 ABY]] on [[Had Abbadon]]<ref name="Legacy31">[[Legacy (2006) 31|''Legacy'' (2006) 31]]</ref>
*[[Atton Rand]]<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*[[Revan/Legends|Revan]]<ref name="Revan" />
*[[Bastila Shan]]<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
*[[Vima Sunrider]] or [[Sunrider family|descendant]]<ref>The Sunrider line survived into the [[New Republic era]], as evidenced by the existence of Vima's descendant [[Vima-Da-Boda]].</ref>
